back_translation: A song. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. For the choirmaster. According to Mahalath Leannoth. A Maskil of Heman the Ezrahite.
O LORD, the God of my salvation, day and night I cry out before You.
Incline Your ear to listen to my cry, and let my prayer come before You.
For my soul is full of troubles, and my life draws near to Sheol.
I am counted among those who go down to the Pit; I am like a man without strength.
I am forsaken among the dead, like the slain who lie in the grave, whom You remember no more, and who are cut off from Your care.
You have laid me in the lowest pit, in the dark places of the depths.
Your wrath lies heavily upon me, and You have overwhelmed me with all Your waves. Selah
You have removed my friends far from me; You have made me repulsive to them; I am confined and cannot escape.
My eyes grow dim from affliction; O LORD, I call to You every day; I spread out my hands to You.
Do You work wonders for the dead? Do the departed spirits rise up to praise You? Selah
Is Your loving devotion proclaimed in the grave, or Your faithfulness in Abaddon?
Are Your wonders known in the darkness, or Your righteousness in the land of oblivion?
But to You, O LORD, I cry for help; in the morning my prayer comes before You.
O LORD, why do You reject me? Why do You hide Your face from me?
From my youth I have been afflicted and near death; I have borne Your terrors, and I am in despair.
Your burning wrath has swept over me; Your terrors have destroyed me.
All day long they surround me like water; they close in on me from every side.
You have taken my beloved and my friend from me; darkness is my closest companion.
